verification_result.notes: All 14 tests pass (8 inline + 6 smoke). Clippy clean (no warnings). rustfmt clean. The crate builds as a standalone package via an empty [workspace] table in its own Cargo.toml, so it does NOT pull the 56+ crates in the root FocalPoint/Phenotype workspace into the build. The 5 variants (Domain, NotFound, Conflict, Validation, Storage) are exactly the L3 DAG's spec; a 6th variant would break the `kind_is_total_over_all_5_variants` tripwire test.

notes: V3 DAG L3 #46 — author canonical pheno-errors Rust crate consolidating the 5 most-common error patterns.

Crate is intentionally STANDALONE (empty [workspace] in its own Cargo.toml), not a member of the root Cargo.toml's [workspace.members] — to avoid touching the in-progress root Cargo.toml that another L3 agent is concurrently modifying, and to keep the new crate's test/build loop independent of the 56-crate root workspace.

The 5 variants borrow their shape from phenoShared/crates/phenotype-error-core (which uses 7 variants in DomainError: Validation, InvariantViolation, NotFound, Duplicate, InvalidStateTransition, NotPermitted, PolicyEvaluation, Other). The new crate flattens to the L3 DAG's canonical 5: Domain, NotFound, Conflict, Validation, Storage. Downstream L5 lanes can re-export from the shared crate to unify.

From impls: std::io::Error -> AppError::Storage, anyhow::Error -> AppError::Domain (walks .chain() to preserve the full causal trail), &'static str -> AppError::Domain, String -> AppError::Domain. Deliberately NO blanket From<E: Error + Send + Sync + 'static> for AppError because that would conflict with the concrete std::io::Error impl under Rust's coherence rules.

anyhow::Context works automatically: anyhow's blanket impl From<E: Error + Send + Sync + 'static> for anyhow::Error covers AppError, so .context() / .with_context() on Result<T, AppError> produces an anyhow::Error that round-trips back to AppError via the explicit From<anyhow::Error> impl.

The kind() method is a stable snake_case tag (Domain, not_found, conflict, validation, storage) suitable for log fields and metrics labels. Do NOT use it as a wire code; phenotype-error-core::ErrorCode is the canonical wire vocabulary.
